question: Which video game franchise features a plumber named Mario?
explanation: Mario is Nintendo's mascot, created by Shigeru Miyamoto in 1981.

question: What is the best-selling video game of all time?
explanation: Minecraft has sold over 300 million copies across all platforms.

question: Which game popularised the 'Battle Royale' genre?
explanation: PUBG (PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds) popularised the battle royale genre when it launched in 2017.

question: In which year was the original PlayStation released in Japan?
explanation: Sony released the original PlayStation in Japan on December 3, 1994.

question: What is the name of Link's nemesis in The Legend of Zelda?
explanation: Ganondorf (or Ganon) is the main villain in most Legend of Zelda games.

question: Fortnite is a free-to-play game.
explanation: Fortnite Battle Royale has been free-to-play since its release in 2017.

question: The original Pac-Man was released in the 1980s.
explanation: Pac-Man was released by Namco in Japan in May 1980.

question: Steam is developed by Microsoft.
explanation: Steam is developed and operated by Valve Corporation, not Microsoft.

question: The Nintendo Switch can be played both as a home console and a handheld device.
explanation: The Nintendo Switch is a hybrid console — it docks to a TV or detaches for handheld play.

question: Sonic the Hedgehog is a Nintendo character.
explanation: Sonic is Sega's mascot, created to rival Nintendo's Mario.

question: "It's dangerous to go alone! Take ___"
explanation: Famous line from The Legend of Zelda (1986) — the old man gives Link a sword.

question: In Among Us, players vote to ___ suspected impostors.
explanation: Players vote to eject crewmates they suspect of being impostors.

question: In Minecraft, you need a ___ pickaxe to mine diamonds.
explanation: You need at least an iron pickaxe to successfully mine diamond ore in Minecraft.

question: The main character of the Halo series is called Master ___.
explanation: Master Chief (John-117) is the protagonist of the Halo franchise by Bungie/343 Industries.

question: In chess-like strategy game, the most powerful piece is the ___.
explanation: The queen is the most powerful piece in chess, able to move in any direction.

explanation: Nintendo 64 (1996) → Xbox 360 (2005) → PlayStation 4 (2013)

explanation: Super Mario World (1990) → Super Mario 64 (1996) → Super Mario Odyssey (2017)

question: In what year was the first Call of Duty game released?
explanation: The original Call of Duty was released on October 29, 2003 for PC.

question: How many copies has Minecraft sold across all platforms (in millions)?
explanation: Minecraft surpassed 300 million copies sold across all platforms, making it the best-selling game of all time.

question: In what year was the original Game Boy released in Japan?
explanation: The original Nintendo Game Boy was released in Japan on April 21, 1989.

question: In what year was Pokémon Red and Blue first released in Japan?
explanation: Pokémon Red and Green were released in Japan on February 27, 1996. Blue followed later the same year.

question: How many main Pokémon existed in the original Generation 1 Pokédex?
explanation: Generation 1 introduced 151 Pokémon, from Bulbasaur (#001) to Mew (#151).

question: Which company made the Xbox console?
explanation: Microsoft launched the original Xbox in 2001.

question: What colour is the ghost Blinky in the original Pac-Man?
explanation: Blinky is the red ghost in Pac-Man, known for directly chasing the player.

question: In which game would you find the 'Konami Code'?
explanation: The Konami Code (↑↑↓↓←→←→BA) became famous in Contra (1988) for giving 30 lives.

question: Which gaming platform hosts the annual 'The International' Dota 2 tournament?
explanation: Dota 2 and The International are PC-exclusive, organized by Valve.

question: Which game features the Vault Boy mascot?
explanation: The Vault Boy is the mascot of the Fallout series, featured on perks and promotional material.

question: What is the name of the princess that Mario rescues in most games?
explanation: Princess Peach is the ruler of the Mushroom Kingdom and Mario's primary motivation in most games.

question: Name the open-world survival game developed by Mojang.
explanation: Minecraft was created by Markus 'Notch' Persson and released by Mojang in 2011.

question: What is the name of the currency used in The Legend of Zelda games?
explanation: Rupees are the in-game currency throughout most Zelda titles, often found in grass or chests.

question: In which game does the character 'Master Chief' appear?
explanation: Master Chief (John-117) is the protagonist of the Halo franchise, first appearing in Halo: Combat Evolved (2001).

question: What studio developed The Last of Us?
explanation: Naughty Dog, a Sony first-party studio, developed The Last of Us, released in 2013 for PlayStation 3.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Mario, Nintendo's iconic plumber, first appeared in Donkey Kong (1981) as 'Jumpman' and has since starred in over 200 games, making him the most recognised video game character.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Link is the protagonist of Nintendo's Legend of Zelda series. He is often confused with Zelda, who is actually the princess he saves.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Master Chief (John-117) is the protagonist of Microsoft's Halo franchise, one of the best-selling Xbox exclusive video game series.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Pikachu is Pokémon's mascot and the most recognisable Pokémon. It first appeared in Pokémon Red and Blue (1996) and is Ash's partner in the anime.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Lara Croft is the archaeologist protagonist of the Tomb Raider series by Core Design/Crystal Dynamics, first appearing in 1996.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Sonic the Hedgehog is Sega's mascot, introduced in 1991 as a rival to Nintendo's Mario. Known for his supersonic speed and attitude.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Kratos is the protagonist of Sony's God of War series, a Spartan warrior who battles gods. The 2018 reboot reimagined him in Norse mythology.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Geralt of Rivia is the protagonist of CD Projekt Red's The Witcher series, based on Andrzej Sapkowski's Polish fantasy novels.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Pac-Man (1980, Namco) is one of the most iconic arcade games ever made. The character is a circle that eats dots while being chased by four colourful ghosts.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Cloud Strife is the protagonist of Final Fantasy VII (1997), Square Enix's most celebrated RPG. He wields the iconic Buster Sword.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Samus Aran is the bounty hunter protagonist of Nintendo's Metroid series (1986). One of gaming's earliest female protagonists.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Solid Snake is the protagonist of Konami's Metal Gear series, created by Hideo Kojima. Famous for his stealth missions and the iconic '!' alert sound.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Banjo-Kazooie is a bear-and-bird duo from Rare's 1998 Nintendo 64 3D platformer, widely regarded as one of the greatest games of the era.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Ryu is the iconic protagonist of Capcom's Street Fighter series (1987), known for his signature moves Hadouken and Shoryuken.

question: Which video game character is this?
explanation: Donkey Kong is Nintendo's giant gorilla, first appearing as the villain of the 1981 arcade game. He later became the hero of his own platformer series.

explanation: Shadow Moses Island is the secret nuclear weapons facility where the game takes place.

explanation: The Nintendo 64 launched in 1996.

question: Which company created the Xbox?
explanation: Microsoft launched the Xbox in 2001.

question: What processor powered the Sega Mega Drive / Genesis?
explanation: The Mega Drive ran a Motorola 68000 at 7.67 MHz as its main CPU, with a secondary Zilog Z80 handling sound and backward compatibility with the Master System.

question: Which chip handled audio processing on the Super Nintendo?
explanation: The Sony SPC700 was an 8-bit processor dedicated to audio in the SNES, capable of 8 channels of sampled sound with built-in DSP effects.

question: Which NES game is infamous for its brutally difficult 'Turbo Tunnel' level?
explanation: The Turbo Tunnel in Battletoads (1991) requires perfect memorisation of dozens of obstacles at blinding speed and is considered one of gaming's hardest levels.

question: The Atari 2600's CPU was a cost-reduced variant of which chip?
explanation: The Atari 2600 used a MOS Technology 6507 — a 6502 with a smaller 13-bit address bus — running at 1.19 MHz. Cost-cutting removed interrupt lines and reduced addressable memory.

question: Which SNES game was the first to use the Super FX graphics coprocessor?
explanation: Star Fox (1993) was the first SNES game to use the Super FX chip, enabling polygon-based 3D graphics not otherwise possible on the base hardware.

question: What was the standard ROM size of early Atari 2600 cartridges?
explanation: Original Atari 2600 cartridges were limited to 4 KB of ROM. Bank switching was later developed by ingenious programmers to allow larger games like Pitfall II.

question: Which company co-developed the TurboGrafx-16 (PC Engine) alongside NEC?
explanation: NEC and Hudson Soft jointly developed the PC Engine, released in Japan in 1987. Hudson designed the CPU (HuC6280) and GPU while NEC handled manufacturing.

question: What was the Sega Mega Drive's secondary processor, used for sound and Master System backward compatibility?
explanation: The Mega Drive's secondary Zilog Z80 handled FM sound chip communication and could run Master System software via the Power Base Converter add-on.

question: Which Treasure-developed Mega Drive game features a puppet protagonist who detaches and throws his own head?
explanation: Dynamite Headdy (1994) stars Headdy, a marionette who fires his detachable head as a weapon and collects different head power-ups that change his abilities.

question: Approximately how much did the SNK Neo Geo AES home console cost at US launch in 1990?
explanation: The Neo Geo AES launched at $649.99 — the most expensive home console of its era by far. Individual game cartridges could cost over $200 each.

question: What is the name of the alien final boss in the SNES RPG Earthbound (Mother 2)?
explanation: Giygas is Earthbound's terrifying final villain — an incomprehensible alien entity defeated only through prayer. Its abstract, disturbing visual design remains iconic.

question: Which game was bundled with the Sega Mega Drive at launch in Japan and let players transform into powerful animals?
explanation: Altered Beast (1988) was the Mega Drive's pack-in launch game. Players collect spirit balls to morph into a werewolf, bear, golden wolf or dragon.

question: What peripheral did Nintendo bundle with early NES Deluxe Sets to present the console as a toy rather than a video game system?
explanation: After the 1983 video game crash, Nintendo bundled R.O.B. with the NES to market it as a toy to sceptical US retailers. Only two games ever used R.O.B.: Gyromite and Stack-Up.

question: Which Treasure game for the Mega Drive consists almost entirely of rapid boss battles and is notorious for its extreme difficulty?
explanation: Alien Soldier (1995) by Treasure features 25 bosses with almost no filler levels. Gamefan magazine called it 'possibly the hardest game ever created.'

question: What was the screen resolution of the original Nintendo Game Boy?
explanation: The original Game Boy's LCD was 160×144 pixels, displaying 4 shades of grey-green. Despite its limitations it dominated the handheld market against the more powerful Game Gear and Lynx.

question: The Atari 5200 was natively backward compatible with Atari 2600 cartridges.
explanation: False. The 5200 was not backward compatible out of the box — a separate adapter was sold separately. Many owners found this frustrating given the large existing 2600 library.

question: The SNES was the first mainstream home console controller to feature L and R shoulder buttons.
explanation: True. Nintendo introduced shoulder buttons on the SNES controller in 1990, a design element now standard on virtually every controller.

question: The Sega Mega Drive used a Yamaha FM synthesizer chip for sound.
explanation: True. The Yamaha YM2612 6-channel FM synthesizer gave the Mega Drive a rich, distinctive sound, famously used in Streets of Rage, Sonic and many other titles.

question: Chrono Trigger was developed by a team that included Hironobu Sakaguchi, Yuji Horii and Dragon Ball artist Akira Toriyama.
explanation: True. This 'Dream Team' collaboration produced what is widely regarded as one of the greatest RPGs ever made.

question: The Sega Game Gear had a larger, colour, backlit screen compared to the original monochrome Game Boy.
explanation: True. Game Gear had a 3.2-inch colour backlit screen vs Game Boy's 2.6-inch green monochrome. The trade-off: Game Gear drained 6 AA batteries in about 3-5 hours.

question: The SNK Neo Geo AES home cartridges were identical to those used in the MVS arcade hardware.
explanation: True. The AES and MVS shared identical hardware and ROM boards, which is why home cartridges were extremely expensive — they were essentially arcade boards for home use.

question: The ColecoVision was released before the Atari 5200.
explanation: True. ColecoVision launched in August 1982, three months before the Atari 5200 in November 1982. ColecoVision's superior graphics and Donkey Kong port helped it sell over 2 million units.

question: Nintendo 64 cartridges could hold more data than PlayStation CD-ROMs.
explanation: False. N64 cartridges maxed out at around 64 MB while a PlayStation CD-ROM held ~650 MB. This storage gap is a key reason Final Fantasy VII switched from Nintendo to Sony.

question: In Chrono Trigger, the frog knight's real human name is ______.
explanation: Glenn was transformed into a frog-knight by the wizard Magus. He wields the legendary Masamune sword and is one of the most beloved characters in the game.

question: The Sega Mega Drive was released in North America under the name Sega ______.
explanation: The Mega Drive was rebranded as the Genesis for North America because 'Mega Drive' was already trademarked by a US company.

question: Using the Konami Code in Contra on the NES grants players ______ extra lives.
explanation: ↑↑↓↓←→←→BA grants 30 lives in Contra — widely considered essential to completing the game without prior memorisation of the levels.

question: The Atari 2600 was originally sold as the Atari VCS, where VCS stands for Video Computer ______.
explanation: The VCS (Video Computer System) name was dropped in 1982 when the Atari 5200 launched and Atari needed to distinguish the older console.

question: In Streets of Rage 2, Adam Hunter is replaced by a massive wrestler named Max ______.
explanation: Max Thunder is a power-focused brawler added to the Streets of Rage 2 roster. Adam was kidnapped and became a plot device, leaving the fight to Axel, Blaze, Skate and Max.

question: In the Mega Drive game Comix Zone, the comic book artist protagonist is named Sketch ______.
explanation: Sketch Turner is an artist sucked into his own comic book by his villain creation Mortus in Comix Zone (1995) — a game noted for its unique panel-to-panel movement mechanic.

question: Super Metroid on the SNES takes place on the alien planet ______.
explanation: Planet Zebes is the labyrinthine setting of Super Metroid (1994), home to the Space Pirates and Mother Brain. Its nonlinear design pioneered the 'Metroidvania' genre.

question: The NES game Mega Man 2 features a memorable robot master named Metal ______.
explanation: Metal Man is one of Mega Man 2's eight robot masters, known for throwing Metal Blades — widely considered the most overpowered weapon in the game.

question: What was the name of Sega's 32-bit add-on peripheral for the Mega Drive/Genesis?
explanation: The Sega 32X (1994) plugged into the Mega Drive's cartridge slot and added dual SH2 processors. It was a commercial failure with only 36 games, discontinued within a year.

question: Name the futuristic racing SNES launch title that used Mode 7 to simulate 3D track scaling.
explanation: F-Zero (1990) used Mode 7 to rotate and scale a flat track texture, creating the illusion of speed and depth. It remains one of the SNES's most impressive technical showcases.

question: Name the alien planet-parasite that serves as the final boss in Chrono Trigger.
explanation: Lavos is a planet-draining alien that has been living inside the Earth for 65 million years. Players can actually fight it as early as 12,000 BC if they choose.

question: Name the 1988 Capcom NES game where the hero uses a bionic grappling arm instead of jumping to traverse levels.
explanation: Bionic Commando (1988) is notable for having no jump button — players must swing and climb using a mechanical grappling arm in a grid of interconnected areas.

question: What NES floor-mat peripheral let players control games by running and jumping on its pressure-sensitive pads?
explanation: The Power Pad (1986) was a large floor mat with 12 pressure-sensitive buttons used for sports games like Stadium Events and World Class Track Meet.

question: Name the CD-ROM add-on peripheral Atari released for the Jaguar in 1995.
explanation: The Atari Jaguar CD (1995) added CD-ROM capabilities and sold fewer than 20,000 units. Atari exited the hardware business in 1996 and never released another console.

question: In what year was the Atari 2600 first released?
explanation: The Atari 2600 launched on September 11, 1977, originally as the Atari VCS, priced at $199. It went on to sell over 30 million units.

question: In what year did the Super Famicom (SNES) launch in Japan?
explanation: The Super Famicom launched in Japan on November 21, 1990, selling 300,000 units on its first day despite being allocated only through lottery in some areas.

question: How many bits was the Sega Mega Drive / Genesis marketed as?
explanation: The Mega Drive was a 16-bit console. Sega's marketing aggressively promoted this with slogans like 'Genesis does what Nintendon't.'

question: Approximately how many officially licensed games were released for the NES in North America?
explanation: The NES had approximately 700 officially licensed games in North America, released between 1985 and 1994.

question: In what year was the original Nintendo Game Boy released in North America?
explanation: The Game Boy launched in North America on July 31, 1989, bundled with Tetris — a pairing that made it an instant bestseller.

question: Rank these Sega consoles by Japanese launch year (oldest first):
explanation: Mega Drive (October 1988) → Saturn (November 1994) → Dreamcast (November 1998)

question: Rank these NES games by Japanese release year (oldest first):
explanation: Super Mario Bros (September 1985) → Mega Man 2 (December 1988) → Castlevania III (September 1989)

question: Rank these consoles by main CPU RAM amount (smallest to largest):
explanation: Atari 2600 had only 128 bytes of RAM; NES had 2 KB; SNES had 128 KB of work RAM — each generation a dramatic leap in available memory.

question: Rank these SNES RPGs by Japanese release year (oldest first):
explanation: Final Fantasy IV (July 1991) → Secret of Mana (August 1993) → Chrono Trigger (March 1995)

question: What was the direct home console successor to the Sega Master System?
explanation: The Sega Mega Drive (1988) succeeded the 8-bit Master System and brought 16-bit gaming to homes.

question: Which Atari console released in 1993 was controversially marketed as '64-bit'?
explanation: The Atari Jaguar (1993) was marketed as the first 64-bit console. Its actual architecture was debated — critics argued it was closer to two 32-bit chips working together.

question: The PSP's full name stands for PlayStation ______.
The original Game Boy launched in Japan on April 21, 1989. What was bundled with Japanese Game Boys at launch instead of Tetris?
The Sega Nomad, released in 1995, was a handheld version of which home console that could play its cartridges natively?
The Nintendo DS lite was slimmer than the original DS and retained backward compatibility with GBA games via its Slot-2. How much lighter was the DS Lite compared to the original DS?
Elden Ring (2022) was developed by which studio?
explanation: FromSoftware developed Elden Ring, with Bandai Namco publishing it.

question: Which famous author co-created the lore for Elden Ring?
explanation: George R.R. Martin, author of Game of Thrones, wrote the mythos for Elden Ring.

question: What is the name of Kratos's son in God of War (2018) and Ragnarök?
explanation: Atreus, also known as Loki in Norse mythology, is Kratos's son and companion.

question: Hogwarts Legacy is set in the 1800s, long before the events of the Harry Potter books.
explanation: Hogwarts Legacy takes place in the late 1800s, roughly 100 years before Harry Potter's story.

question: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom is the direct sequel to which game?
explanation: Tears of the Kingdom (2023) is the direct sequel to Breath of the Wild (2017).

question: In Marvel's Spider-Man 2 (2023), who is the second playable Spider-Man alongside Peter Parker?
explanation: Miles Morales and Peter Parker are both playable in Marvel's Spider-Man 2.

question: Baldur's Gate 3 was developed by which studio?
explanation: Larian Studios, also known for the Divinity: Original Sin series, developed Baldur's Gate 3.

question: In Palworld (2024), what are the collectible creatures called?
explanation: Palworld revolves around collecting and training creatures called Pals.

question: Helldivers 2 (2024) has players fighting for which fictional government?
explanation: In Helldivers 2, players are soldiers of Super Earth, spreading managed democracy across the galaxy.

question: Who is the main protagonist of Final Fantasy XVI (2023)?
explanation: Clive Rosfield is the adult protagonist of Final Fantasy XVI, set in the world of Valisthea.

question: Cyberpunk 2077 is set in which fictional city?
explanation: Night City, a sprawling dystopian metropolis in California, is the setting of Cyberpunk 2077.

question: In Stray (2022), what animal does the player control?
explanation: Stray is an adventure game where you play as a stray cat navigating a post-apocalyptic city.

question: What unique mechanic defines Hi-Fi Rush (2023)?
options: Everything attacks in sync with the music beat
explanation: Hi-Fi Rush syncs all gameplay — attacks, enemies, and the world — to the beat of the music.

question: It Takes Two won Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2021.
explanation: It Takes Two by Hazelight Studios won GOTY at The Game Awards 2021, beating out other major titles.

question: What is the open-world region called in Pokémon Legends: Arceus (2022)?
explanation: Hisui is an ancient version of the Sinnoh region, set in feudal-era Japan.

question: Pokémon Scarlet and Violet are set in which region?
explanation: Paldea, inspired by the Iberian Peninsula, is the setting of Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

question: What new ability did Kirby gain in Kirby and the Forgotten Land (2022)?
explanation: Mouthful Mode lets Kirby inhale large objects like cars, traffic cones, and vending machines.

question: What new power-up lets Mario transform into an elephant in Super Mario Bros. Wonder (2023)?
explanation: The Elephant Fruit transforms Mario into Elephant Mario, one of the new power-ups in Wonder.

question: In Metroid Dread (2021), what new robotic enemies relentlessly pursue Samus?
explanation: E.M.M.I. (Extraplanetary Multiform Mobile Identifier) robots are nearly indestructible hunters in Metroid Dread.

question: Vampire Survivors (2022) is best described as which type of game?
explanation: Vampire Survivors is a bullet-hell survivor game where you auto-attack waves of enemies.

question: Diablo IV is set in which dark fantasy world?
explanation: Sanctuary is the world where the eternal conflict between Heaven and Hell takes place in Diablo.

question: Street Fighter 6 (2023) introduced which new combat system replacing V-Gauge?
explanation: The Drive System is Street Fighter 6's new core mechanic, enabling Drive Rush, Parry, Impact and more.

question: Who is the main playable character in Tekken 8 (2024)?
explanation: Jin Kazama is the central protagonist of Tekken 8, continuing his battle against his father Kazuya.

question: Lies of P (2023) is a Soulslike game based on which classic fairy tale character?
explanation: Lies of P reimagines Pinocchio as a dark Soulslike, set in the Belle Époque city of Krat.

question: Astro Bot (2024) is exclusively available on which platform?
explanation: Astro Bot is a PS5-exclusive platformer developed by Team Asobi, the creators of Astro's Playroom.

question: Black Myth: Wukong (2024) is based on which classic Chinese novel?
explanation: Black Myth: Wukong is inspired by Journey to the West, a 16th-century Chinese epic following Sun Wukong.

question: Dragon's Dogma 2 (2024) was developed by which studio?
explanation: Capcom developed Dragon's Dogma 2, the long-awaited sequel to the 2012 original.

question: Forza Horizon 5 (2021) is set in which country?
explanation: Forza Horizon 5 features a stunning open-world recreation of Mexico, its largest map yet.

question: Which studio developed God of War Ragnarök (2022)?
explanation: Santa Monica Studio developed both God of War (2018) and its sequel Ragnarök.

question: What is the name of Elden Ring's 2024 DLC expansion?
explanation: Shadow of the Erdtree, released June 2024, is the only DLC for Elden Ring and received universal acclaim.

question: Hogwarts Legacy allows players to choose their Hogwarts house at the start of the game.
explanation: Players are sorted into one of the four Hogwarts houses: Gryffindor, Slytherin, Ravenclaw, or Hufflepuff.

question: Palworld was developed by which studio?
explanation: PocketPair, a small Japanese indie studio, developed Palworld, which became a massive hit in early 2024.

question: Resident Evil 4 Remake (2023) — who is the main protagonist?
explanation: Leon S. Kennedy returns as the protagonist in the Resident Evil 4 Remake, rescuing the President's daughter.

question: Dead Space Remake (2023) was developed by which studio?
explanation: EA Motive rebuilt Dead Space from the ground up, as the original studio Visceral Games was shut down in 2017.

question: Assassin's Creed Mirage (2023) is set in which city?
explanation: Mirage is set in 9th-century Baghdad and follows Basim Ibn Is'haq, returning to the series' stealth roots.

question: Balatro (2024) is a roguelite deckbuilder based on which card game?
explanation: Balatro uses poker hand rules as its core mechanic, letting players build decks to create overpowered combos.

question: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown (2024) is a 2D Metroidvania developed by which Ubisoft team?
explanation: Ubisoft Montpellier, known for Rayman Origins and Beyond Good & Evil, developed The Lost Crown.

question: In Baldur's Gate 3, what is the maximum number of players supported in co-op?
explanation: Baldur's Gate 3 supports up to 4 players in co-op, matching the standard D&D party size.

question: In Cyberpunk 2077, your customisable protagonist is simply known as ___.
explanation: The player character in Cyberpunk 2077 goes by the nickname V, regardless of their backstory choice.

question: What new ability in Tears of the Kingdom allows Link to reverse the flow of time on objects?
explanation: Recall rewinds the movement of an object, letting Link ride falling rocks back up or reverse projectiles.

question: Alan Wake 2 (2023) is Alan Wake's first direct single-player sequel.
explanation: Alan Wake 2 is the first direct sequel to Alan Wake (2010), released 13 years later by Remedy Entertainment.

question: Sea of Stars (2023) is a turn-based RPG heavily inspired by which classic SNES game?
explanation: Sea of Stars draws heavy inspiration from Chrono Trigger, particularly its combat system and visual style.

question: In which year was the open-world action RPG Elden Ring released?
explanation: Elden Ring was released on February 25, 2022 for PC, PS4, PS5, Xbox One and Xbox Series X/S.

question: Overwatch 2 is a free-to-play game, unlike the original Overwatch which required a purchase.
explanation: Overwatch 2 launched in October 2022 as a free-to-play replacement for the original game.

question: Which of these games won Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2023?
options: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
explanation: Baldur's Gate 3 swept The Game Awards 2023, winning Game of the Year among many other categories.

question: What is the name of the protagonist in Starfield (2023)?
explanation: Like other Bethesda RPGs, Starfield's protagonist is a fully customisable player character with no set name.

question: The Phantom Liberty expansion for Cyberpunk 2077 introduced a new district called ___.
explanation: Dogtown is the new lawless district in Night City added by the Phantom Liberty DLC in 2023.

question: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon (2023) marked FromSoftware's return to the mech series after approximately how many years?
explanation: The last Armored Core entry was Verdict Day in 2013, making Fires of Rubicon a return after about 10 years.

question: In The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom, what ability lets Link pass through ceilings?
explanation: Ascend lets Link swim upward through solid surfaces above him, enabling vertical traversal through structures.

question: Which game won Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2024?
options: Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree
explanation: Astro Bot by Team Asobi won Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2024.
